channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Expected release of high-profile male hostages sparks excitement and anxiety in Israel
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — News that three high-profile hostages are expected to be released Saturday, including the father of the youngest captives held in Gaza, brought excitement and trepidation to Israel on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Divers returning to the Potomac River for DC plane crash recovery and investigation
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Divers are expected to return to the Potomac River on Friday as part of the recovery and investigation after a midair collision killed 67 people in the United States' deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Recovery work continues after deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pioneering Black Army general who lived in El Paso dies at 100
item.description: Retired Army Maj. Gen. Edward Greer, who broke racial barriers during his long Army career, died at age 100 at his El Paso home on Wednesday, Jan. 29, his family said.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, NewsNation

item.title: South Carolina prepares for third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina is preparing to execute the third inmate to be put to death since September as the state goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn't find lethal injection drugs. Marion Bowman Jr.'s execution is scheduled for 6 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: A midair collision between an Army helicopter and an American Airlines flight from Kansas has killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Tara Copp And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Divers returning to the Potomac River for DC plane crash recovery and investigation
item.description: Divers are expected to return to the Potomac River as part of the recovery and investigation after the United States’ deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: Lolita C. Baldor, Tara Copp, Adriana Gomez Licon And Lea Skene, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 12-year-old girl among figure skaters killed in DC plane crash
item.description: A 12-year-old Maryland girl was among the figure skaters who were killed after an American Airlines jet and Army helicopter collided near Washington D.C. Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dick Button, Olympic great and voice of skating, dies at 95
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dick Button was more than the most accomplished men’s figure skater in history. He was one of his sport’s great innovators and promoters, the man responsible not only for inventing the flying camel spin but describing it to generations of fans.
item.dc:creator: BARRY WILNER and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Carmel woman among those killed in D.C. passenger jet, helicopter crash
item.description: (WXIN/WTTV): An Indiana University Alumna was among the 67 people killed when a plane and a military helicopter collided near the Ronald Reagan National Airport in Washington, D.C. Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Collision between passenger jet and Army helicopter near DC leaves no survivors
item.description: A collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport near Washington, D.C, killed 67 people, including more than a dozen figure skaters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge extends court-monitoring agreement for children in Customs and Border Protection custody
item.description: McAllen, Texas (AP) — A federal judge extended a court agreement on Thursday ensuring safe and sanitary conditions for migrant children in federal custody a day after U.S. Customs and Border Protection was set to begin self-monitoring.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Olympian Nancy Kerrigan mourns loss of skating community members after DC plane crash
item.description: “Much like everyone here has been saying is not sure how to process it,” she told MSNBC while in tears on Thursday at the Boston Skating Club.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Honolulu police have arrested 6 people in connection with deadly New Year’s fireworks blast
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Police arrested a total of six people in connection with a deadly explosion of illegal fireworks in a Honolulu neighborhood on New Year’s Eve and confiscated 500 pounds (227 kilograms) of unused fireworks from the scene of the blast, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Neo-Nazis charged with grooming, extorting California children to make pornographic, violent material
item.description: Four members of a neo-Nazi online group have been charged with federal crimes accusing them of grooming and extorting more than a dozen children to produce pornographic media and violent videos of self-harm.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump holds briefing on plane crash near DC, suggests DEI programs contributed
item.description: President Donald Trump held a briefing Thursday morning on the deadly collision between an American Airlines plane and a military helicopter over the Potomac River.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Here's how much Super Bowl viewers are expected to spend on food, drinks
item.description: With the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles gearing up for the Super Bowl LIX, a new LendingTree report found that Americans are planning to splurge more on the big game this year.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Figure skaters among those on flight that collided with Army helicopter
item.description: U.S. Figure Skating released a statement saying athletes, coaches, and family members were on their way home from the National Development Camp held in conjunction with the U.S. Figure Skating Championship in Kansas when the collision happened.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, ABC 4

item.title: 3 soldiers on Black Hawk helicopter involved in DC airliner crash
item.description: The Black Hawk that struck an American Airlines flight landing at Reagan airport had three soldiers on board and no VIPs or senior officials were involved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Teen figure skaters and their coaches, group of hunters and students among victims of air crash
item.description: Passengers aboard the American Airlines flight that collided with an Army helicopter and crashed into the Potomac River included athletes returning from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Video captures fiery crash between American Airlines flight and Army Blackhawk
item.description: Editor's note: The video may appear graphic to some viewers. (NEXSTAR) - Images from the EarthCam video service captured images of what appears to be the fiery crash of an American Airlines jet over the Potomac River Wednesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Refugee resettlement agencies scramble after Trump orders them to halt their federally funded work
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tens of thousands of recently arrived refugees might lose support for basic necessities like food and rent after a Trump administration order suspended federal funding for resettlement agencies.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump makes moves to expand power, sparking chaos, possible constitutional crisis
item.description: Just a little over a week into his second term, President Donald Trump is taking steps to maximize his power, sparking chaos and what critics contend is a constitutional crisis as he challenges the separation of powers that have defined American government for more than 200 years.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Warriors to retire Andre Iguodala's No. 9 jersey
item.description: As Andre Iguodala moves around town in the Bay Area these days, he is regularly stopped by fans eager to praise him for how he carried himself while playing for the Golden State Warriors.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lions agree to hire Broncos' John Morton as OC and promote Kelvin Sheppard to DC, AP source says
item.description: The Detroit Lions have agreed to hire Denver Broncos assistant John Morton as offensive coordinator and promote Kelvin Sheppard to defensive coordinator, according to a person familiar with the situation.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Disturbing messages seen in empty lot in downtown Los Angeles are not nefarious: LAPD
item.description: A patch of undeveloped land in downtown Los Angeles is going viral on social media for some distressing messages seen from above, but police have confirmed that they found no illicit activity in the area.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Study says climate change made conditions that fed California wildfires more likely, more intense
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Human-caused climate change increased the likelihood and intensity of the hot, dry and windy conditions that fanned the flames of the recent devastating Southern California wildfires, a scientific study found.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ousted IG: Trump firings 'a potentially existential threat' to government oversight
item.description: One of 17 government watchdogs ousted by President Trump said that the move was “a potentially existential threat” to a crucial government oversight tool.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Watch a miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off the central coast of California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma proposal requires parents to provide immigration status when enrolling students
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Parents enrolling children in Oklahoma public schools will be required to provide proof of their child’s U.S. citizenship or legal immigration status under a proposed rule approved Tuesday by the State Board of Education.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mariano's hosting company-wide hiring event Thursday
item.description: CHICAGO — Mariano's is hosting a company-wide hiring event at all 44 of its locations on Thursday. According to a press release, the supermarket chain will hold in-person interviews from 2 p.m. to 7 p.m for both full-time and part-time jobs.
item.dc:creator: Marisa Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Weather forces Blue Origin to scrub launch of short space trip
item.description: The payloads were to experience at least two minutes of lunar gravity forces, a first for New Shepard.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, Fox 5

item.title: American Airlines worker struck, killed by vehicle at Charlotte Airport
item.description: A worker at Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) died Monday after being struck by an airline ramp vehicle on the tarmac, local police have confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, WGN 9
